key_insight: The paper turns inverse materials design into a gradient-based optimization problem by embedding reverse-mode automatic differentiation into KKR-CPA so composition can be continuously optimized toward target properties.
engineering_relevance: This enables first-principles discovery of candidate alloys and half-metals across large compositional spaces without relying on massive screening or training datasets.
method: Integrate reverse-mode AD with KKR-CPA electronic-structure calculations.
Treat atomic composition as continuous design variables and compute gradients of any objective.
Use gradient-based optimization to search high-dimensional compositional spaces efficiently.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports successful demonstrations on two case studies, magnetic alloys and half-metals, producing candidate compositions such as (Lu0.553Yb0.447)(Co0.759Fe0.241)2Fe3 and FeZr(Sb0.94Te0.06).
watch_item: The approach depends on the fidelity and differentiability of the underlying KKR-CPA model, so practical performance may be sensitive to physical approximations and numerical stability.
paper_title: A Differentiable DFT-Based Framework for Inverse Materials Design
